primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation de la durée de location d’un bail renouvelable par tacite reconduction ou sans terme défini afin de déterminer l’éligibilité à l’exemption de courte durée d’IFRS 16

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule and selected IFRS 16 because it directly governs lease term determination and the short-term lease exemption for lessees; no other document in the context governs the same eligibility question at a higher or competing level.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is squarely within IFRS 16 because it asks how to assess lease term for purposes of the short-term lease exemption.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the short-term lease exemption and the determination and reassessment of lease term, including enforceability and renewal/termination features.

supporting_authority.reason: These embedded agenda-decision and illustrative paragraphs clarify how to determine lease term for cancellable or renewable leases and how economic incentives affect that assessment.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address useful life and depreciation of leasehold improvements, which may inform economics but do not identify peer accounting approaches for the short-term lease exemption itself.
These paragraphs deal with income taxes and deferred tax, which are not relevant to identifying the lease accounting approaches for this eligibility question.

treatment_families.family: Exemption model for leases that qualify as short-term leases based on the determined lease term
General lessee model when the determined lease term does not support short-term lease treatment

approaches.label: Short-term lease exemption
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 16 provides a distinct lessee accounting model when, after determining the enforceable lease term, the lease qualifies for the short-term exemption.

approaches.label: General lessee lease model
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 16 requires application of the general recognition model when the determined lease term means the lease does not qualify for the short-term exemption.
